question: What specific events could re-rate or further de-rate NVO in the next 6 months?

findings.claim: CagriSema NDA submitted to FDA in December 2025; FDA decision anticipated late 2026.
findings.data: CagriSema (cagrilintide + semaglutide combination) was submitted to US FDA for weight management in Dec 2025, based on REDEFINE 1 and REDEFINE 2 pivotal trials. FDA decision expected late 2026. REDEFINE 1: 22.7% mean weight loss at 68 weeks (3,417 participants). REDEFINE 2: 13.7% in T2D patients.

findings.claim: REDEFINE 4 head-to-head vs tirzepatide FAILED primary endpoint (Feb 23, 2026) — CagriSema 23.0% vs tirzepatide 25.5% weight loss at 84 weeks.
findings.data: Open-label Phase 3 REDEFINE 4 trial: CagriSema 2.4mg/2.4mg achieved 23.0% weight loss at 84 weeks vs tirzepatide 15mg at 25.5%. Primary endpoint of non-inferiority/superiority vs tirzepatide was NOT achieved. JPMorgan downgraded NVO from Overweight to Neutral, slashing CagriSema forecasts by 40-63% for 2027-2030.

findings.claim: REDEFINE 11 (CagriSema 'full weight-loss potential') readout expected H1 2027 — outside 6-month window but the FDA decision on current NDA could come in late 2026.
findings.data: REDEFINE 11 Phase 3 trial exploring CagriSema 2.4/2.4mg 'full weight-loss potential' is expected to report H1 2027. High-dose CagriSema 2.4mg/7.2mg phase 3 trial planned to initiate H2 2026. The late-2026 FDA decision on current CagriSema NDA is a key binary catalyst. If approved, it gives NVO a combination product; if rejected or delayed, it confirms pipeline weakness narrative.
